exemplar.title: How effective has Lululemons focus on corporate social responsibility initiatives been in enhancing its brand performance?

detailedExemplarData.title: How effective has Lululemons focus on corporate social responsibility initiatives been in enhancing its brand performance?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Maintains an ethical/sustainability lens consistently across sections
Integrates CSR concepts into STEEPLE and financial ratio analysis
Clear analytical connections between CSR initiatives and brand reputation
weaknesses: Key concept is not explicitly named as an IB concept
Occasional drift into generic assertions without explicit ties to ethics/sustainability
Diversity statistics lack analysis of their impact on performance
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The submission demonstrates a strong understanding of the key concept, with clear and mostly integrated analysis throughout. However, the concept is not explicitly named as one of the IB key concepts and some sections veer into description without fully tying back to ethics or sustainability, justifying a band-4 score.

strengths: Uses five supporting documents, meeting the 3â5 requirement
All documents relate directly to Lululemonâs CSR and financial data
Primary data (impact reports) provide credible evidence
weaknesses: Depth of document analysis is moderate rather than expansive
Relies on headline figures without deeper critical evaluation
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student provides the recommended number of relevant documents, drawing on primary corporate sources. Depth is sufficient but not wide-ranging or critically triangulated, placing the work in band-3 for relevance and depth.

strengths: Appropriate mix of STEEPLE analysis and profitability ratios
Correct application of net profit margin formula
Tools are clearly linked to aspects of the research question
weaknesses: Application is sometimes descriptive rather than analytical
Selection of tools is adequate but not particularly broad
Force-field analysis is mentioned without prior methodology integration
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The toolkitâSTEEPLE and ratio analysisâis adequately selected and applied with clear relevance. However, the application lacks depth on occasion and the theoretical range is moderate, consistent with a band-3 rating.
feedback.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

strengths: Insightful evaluation of greenwashing risks and reputational impact
Balanced integration of qualitative and quantitative evidence
Solid use of revenue and margin figures to support analysis
weaknesses: Some causal claims (CSR â financial performance) lack controls or evidence
Environmental and social factors could include more metrics
Analysis occasionally overlooks broader market context
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The analysis goes beyond description to evaluate risks and link CSR to performance with generally effective integration. Minor gaps in causal substantiation and contextual metrics prevent top marks, placing it at band-4.

strengths: Conclusions are fully consistent with the presented evidence
Explicitly answers the research question on CSRâs impact
Acknowledges limitations and calls for further research
weaknesses: Recommendations are general and could be more action-oriented
Implications for practice are not deeply explored
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The conclusion directly addresses the research question and aligns with the analysis, including sound reflection on limitations. Its clarity and consistency justify the maximum band-3 score in this criterion.

strengths: Logical organization with clear introduction, methodology, findings, conclusion
Comprehensive table of contents and consistent headings throughout
weaknesses: Transitions between some sections feel abrupt
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The report exhibits an appropriate and coherent structure throughout, with effective navigation and organization, meriting the full band-2 score.

strengths: Includes all required IA elements (title, word count, headings, references)
weaknesses: Notation in tables could be more precise
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All presentation requirements are met and support clarity. Minor inconsistencies in citation style and notation do not detract from overall professionalism, earning the full band-2 score.

strandSelections.reasoning: The report follows a logical order (introduction-methods-findings-conclusion) and includes a table of contents with page numbers. Headings and sub-headings are used consistently, so âappropriate structure throughoutâ (band 2) applies.

**Relevance:** All documents relate directly to Lululemonâs CSR activity or financial performance.  
**Depth:** Most are annual or impact reports, providing primary data; however, only headline figures/page references are extracted, and critical triangulation is limited.  
Thus the evidence is ârelevant and sufficiently in-depthâ rather than demonstrably wide-ranging, placing it in band 3.
All required IA elementsâtitle, word count, contents page, section headings and referencesâare present, so the highest band for presentation is merited.

Five numbered reference documents are used consistently, satisfying the rubric requirement of 3â5 supporting documents; the student does not exceed the limit. Hence the highest band for quantity is appropriate.

Both STEEPLE and ratio analysis are applied directly to the research question, and links to brand performance are drawn; however, application is often descriptive and sometimes leaps to causation without proof. Relevance is clear but effectiveness only moderate, matching band 3.

Key numerical data (revenues, margins, gender ratios) are selected and used in calculations.  Coverage is adequate but not extensiveâonly three years of figures are analysed and some calculations (e.g., gross profit formula) are partly shown. Hence âadequate selection and useâ, band 3.

The narrative moves beyond description: it evaluates green-washing risks, links CSR efforts to financial outcomes, and acknowledges attribution limitations. Integration across qualitative and quantitative evidence is generally effective, though not sustained at the highest level. Band 4 (âmostly effective analysis with some integrationâ) is appropriate.

The student does explicitly answer the research question, asserting that CSR has enhanced brand performance while recognising limitations. Therefore the highest available band (explicit answer) is justified.

The student selects STEEPLE and simple profitability ratios (and mentions Force-field in passing). This constitutes an adequate, though not especially broad or sophisticated, toolkit. Therefore band 3 (âadequate selectionâ) is most fitting.

The conclusion restates findings and is aligned with the evidence cited; caveats are acknowledged but do not contradict earlier analysis. This satisfies âfully consistent with evidenceâ, hitting the top band for this strand.

**Strengths:** The whole report is anchored in Lululemonâs own dataâImpact Report pages are cited, specific revenue/profit figures are calculated, and green-washing allegations are evaluated in terms of potential fines.  
**Weaknesses:**  Some parts rely on generic assertions (âstrong market positionâ, âenhanced reputationâ) without evidence, and the toolsâ discussion sometimes treats CSR impacts in general rather than as Lululemon-specific.  
**Judgement:** Clear analytical connection is present and mostly integrated, matching band 4.

**Strengths:** The student keeps the lens of ethics/sustainability at the centre of every section: the introduction defines CSR, the STEEPLE repeatedly returns to ethical/environmental dimensions, and the financial ratios are explicitly interpreted in the light of ethical reputation.  
**Weaknesses:**  The concept is not explicitly named as one of the IB key concepts (ethics or sustainability) and the link occasionally drifts (e.g., detailed gender-diversity figures are reported without tying them back to the guiding concept).  
**Judgement:** Overall a strong, but not fully seamless, integration; therefore band 4 fits best (âstrong understanding with partially integrated analysisâ).

annotations.comment: The CSR definition remains generic; the student should explicitly tie CSR concepts back to either ethics or sustainability to strengthen key-concept integration.
The identification of greenwashing allegations demonstrates incisive risk awareness and adds depth to the reputational analysis.
Contextualize the potential $400 million fine relative to Lululemonâs annual revenue to assess the materiality of greenwashing risks.
The âBe Wellâ program description is comprehensive, but reliance on corporate sources limits triangulation; include independent assessments.
Effective selection of revenue and profit growth figures provides a solid quantitative basis for evaluating performance trends.
Diversity statistics are descriptive but lack analysis of how representation metrics translate into brand equity or performance.
The social factor section asserts consumer preference shifts; bolster this with specific survey figures or marketâresearch citations.
The technological factor discussion lacks metrics on sustainableâmaterial adoption; include percentages or costâbenefit figures to substantiate claims.
Linking strong financial performance directly to CSR costs and global expansion requires evidence; distinguish correlation from causation.
Balanced discussion of greenwashing allegations adds depth to environmentalâfactor analysis and acknowledges reputational credibility.
The politicalârisk discussion would be stronger with quantified estimates or precedent cases of regulatory sanctions to illustrate potential impact.
Linking fair-labor accreditation to trust effectively highlights how legal compliance underpins brand reputation.
The research question is clearly articulated, focusing precisely on CSRâs impact on brand performance, which establishes a well-defined investigative scope.
The table of contents provides a clear roadmap with consistent numbering and headings, enhancing navigability and structural coherence.
The introduction offers concise contextual background on Lululemonâs history and positioning, effectively grounding the reader in the organizational setting.
While the three CSR initiatives are listed, the student should explicitly link each (âBe Humanâ, âBe Planetâ, âBe Wellâ) to specific brand performance indicators.
The combined use of STEEPLE analysis and profitability ratios demonstrates an appropriate mix of qualitative and quantitative tools.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Mention of force-field analysis in the conclusion lacks preceding demonstration; integrate its application in the methodology section for coherence.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: When discussing environmental targets, critically evaluate the feasibility of achieving a 60% emissions reduction using interim progress data.
The circularity initiative description is vivid, but the analysis should connect how this program affects brand loyalty or financial outcomes.
Explore potential conflicts between transparency in CSR initiatives and competitive advantage to enrich the ethicalâfactor analysis.
Table displaying the gross profit formula uses plain text; employing LaTeX notation (e.g., $$\frac{Gross\;Profit}{Net\;Sales} \times 100$$) would improve clarity.
The gross profit margin table accurately presents percentages but would benefit from brief commentary on year-on-year drivers of change.
The narrative attributes margin improvements solely to CSR; recommend tempering causal claims or providing controls for other factors.
The net profit margin equation is clearly presented, demonstrating correct formula application and enhancing quantitative transparency.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Ensure consistent decimal precision in the net profit margin table to match the gross margin format and aid comparability.
The margin trend analysis overlooks broader market influences; discuss competitor pricing or macroeconomic shifts to contextualize fluctuations.
Force-field analysis is cited in the conclusion but not applied earlier; integrate its frameworks and results in the methodology and findings.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The limitations section provides thoughtful reflection on data gaps and attribution challenges, demonstrating sound evaluative insight.
The concluding statement appropriately calls for further investigation to strengthen causal links, underscoring academic rigor.
Some references are truncated and formatting is inconsistent; ensure complete URLs and uniform citation style to enhance presentation.
